self_report.reflection: Substitute t = u^2/a to get I = (2/sqrt(a)) ∫_0^∞ e^{-(u^2 + a^2/u^2)} du. The classical Glasser/averaging trick with w = u - a/u gives ∫_0^∞ e^{-(u^2+a^2/u^2)} du = (√π/2) e^{-2a}. Hence I = √(π/a) e^{-2a}, matching the Claim exactly.
self_report.notes: Standard Putnam 1985 B-5; substitution plus the u ↔ a/u symmetry trick reproduces the asserted closed form exactly.
